nodes.body:
Should the operation value be an enum?
From the example, I'm guessing this is the CREATE, UPDATE, etc. one
Yeah I think I am going to change it to an enum in the spec too. FYI @dmcwhorter-ddl
What's the expected behavior for `getModelDeploymentById` if there are multiple versions?

Just created another story for it to address it properly. We will have to record multiple versions so that we can recall them properly. I recorded this in https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-55483

Should this exclude archived deployments? Iirc you can use `dao.findById` or something like that if you don't need to fetch the archived ones. Maybe also make it clear in the method name if you intend to include archived deployments.
Does this delete the old version?
No. We don't want to delete old versions for now.

You can probably do something like below to avoid duplication:

```
val query = BsonDocument("isArchived" -> false)
Seq(nameQuery, registeredModelsQuery).flatten.foreach(criteria => $and(criteria :+ query))
```
I'm not sure if we differentiate between archived vs non-existent in other parts of the code. Not a big deal either way.

I actually pulled this from the way we do models, specifically the modelapirepository - unsafeRetrieve method. I think for now this should be ok because we are still figuring out how we want model deployments to behave. I may remove this in the next sprint 👍
